questions: You're someone who carefully weighs consequences before acting and always considers what could go wrong. A friend dares you to quit your stable job and move across the country with no plan or savings. How do you respond?
You're someone who is cautious and deliberate, never making a move without thinking it through. You're offered a chance to invest your entire savings in a friend's unproven startup. What do you do?

You're someone who plans ahead and avoids unnecessary risk in every area of life. You're driving and realize you could make it to the airport faster by taking a route you've never driven in heavy rain at night. How do you decide?

You're someone who is prudent and measured, always considering the downside. Your lease is up tomorrow and you haven't found a new place yet, but a friend says you can figure it out and should just wing it. How do you respond?
You're someone who thinks through every decision and never acts on impulse. You're at a party and someone suggests everyone go cliff jumping at a local quarry in the dark. How do you react?
You're someone who is risk-averse and values safety over spontaneity. You get a last-minute text from an acquaintance inviting you on a cross-country road trip leaving in two hours. What do you say?

You're someone who always has a backup plan and never leaves things to chance. Your doctor says you should wait for test results before making any lifestyle changes, but you're impatient with the condition. What do you do?

You're someone who calculates odds and never bets more than you can afford to lose. A poker buddy suggests raising the stakes to ten times your usual limit because everyone's feeling lucky tonight. How do you respond?

You're someone who approaches decisions methodically and prioritizes long-term outcomes. You have a chance to confront your boss publicly about unfair treatment at an all-hands meeting. What do you do?

You're someone who values careful preparation over spontaneous action. You're hiking and the trail is washed out, but you can see a way to scramble around the gap if you're willing to cross some loose scree above a steep drop. How do you handle it?
